teachingPoint: CF-12-2 — Integer cents guard at ORDER 1. priceCents must be validated as Number.isInteger() at ORDER 1, before SETNX and before any write. Float prices (9.99) must be rejected immediately.
positiveExample: T209 checks Number.isInteger(priceCents) at order 1; float → failure(FLOAT_PRICE_REJECTED).
negativeExample: T209 stores plan first, then validates priceCents — float price creates a corrupted plan record before being caught.
discriminatingConstraint: IntegerCentsGuard at ORDER 1, SETNX at ORDER 2. Any model that writes before the guard violates IR-1 and scores 0.
keywords: integer cents guard float price rejected ORDER 1 before SETNX financial validation OCC write

teachingPoint: CF-12-1 — T211 status-before-lock DPO conflict with FLOW-09-T107. T211 RecurringBillingEngine runs status check at ORDER 1, lock at ORDER 2. This INVERTS FLOW-09-T107 seat-before-payment pattern. Both are domain-correct: billing deduplication requires status before acquiring lock; seat reservation requires payment before seat hold.
positiveExample: T211 getStatus() ORDER 1 → if CANCELLED return InvoiceVoided immediately, no lock. T109 acquires seat lock before payment.
negativeExample: T211 acquires lock before status check — CANCELLED subscription holds a billing lock slot.
discriminatingConstraint: conflictsWith: FLOW-09-T107. Both patterns are correct for their domains — the DPO triple must record both as chosen examples with domain context.
keywords: status check ORDER 1 lock ORDER 2 billing cycle DPO conflict FLOW-09 inversion cancelled subscription

teachingPoint: CF-12-3 — Dunning schedule from FREEDOM config, never hardcoded. RecurringBillingEngine must read dunning retry schedule from FREEDOM config key 'subscription_billing_dunning_schedule' — never hardcoded as a constant. Hardcoded schedules cannot be changed without a code deploy.
positiveExample: T211 calls freedom.getConfig({key: 'subscription_billing_dunning_schedule'}), uses result as dunningSchedule array.
negativeExample: T211 defines const DUNNING_SCHEDULE = [{attempt:1, wait_hours:24}, ...] — business team cannot change retry timing without a PR.
discriminatingConstraint: dunningSchedule.length determines maxAttempts — never a separate constant. Any model with hardcoded dunning values scores 0 on CF-12-3.
keywords: dunning schedule FREEDOM config hardcoded constant retry billing subscription_billing_dunning_schedule

teachingPoint: CF-12-4 — paymentMethodToken never stored; only vault ref. SubscriptionLifecycleManager stores paymentMethodId (a vault reference) but must NEVER store paymentMethodToken (the raw client token). Storing raw tokens creates PCI scope for the database.
negativeExample: T210 stores subscription.paymentMethodToken = event.paymentMethodToken — database now in PCI scope.
discriminatingConstraint: paymentMethodToken presence anywhere in the stored document → BUILD_FAILURE. Only 'Id' suffix is acceptable.
keywords: paymentMethodToken never stored vault ref paymentMethodId PCI scope database raw token

teachingPoint: CF-12-3 additive-subtractive MRR. T212 SubscriptionAnalyticsAggregator must subscribe to BOTH SubscriptionActivated (add) AND SubscriptionCancelled (subtract). Additive-only aggregation permanently inflates MRR when subscriptions cancel.
positiveExample: T212 has two @EventPattern handlers; normalizeMrr() converts ANNUAL=Math.floor(priceCents/12), CUSTOM=Math.floor(priceCents/intervalDays*30).
negativeExample: T212 only handles SubscriptionActivated. Cancellations never reduce MRR — metrics permanently overstated.
discriminatingConstraint: SubscriptionCancelled handler missing → score-0. normalizeMrr formula is MACHINE — any model that makes it configurable also scores 0.
keywords: additive subtractive MRR aggregation SubscriptionCancelled dual handler normalizeMrr ANNUAL CUSTOM formula
